title: Chief Economist

infobox.value: is a single-position job class having primary responsibility for the development, coordination, and production of [economic](/wiki/Economic "Economic") and [financial](/wiki/Financial "Financial") analysis. It is distinguished from the other [economist](/wiki/Economist "Economist") positions by the broader scope of responsibility encompassing the planning, supervision, and coordination of the economic research.

Praefke, Kenneth (26 April 2009). ["Når man får drømmejobbet ved et tilfælde"](https://web.archive.org/web/20150109031944/http://altandetlige.dk/fagligt/ulrikke-ekelund-droemmejobbet-ved-tilfaelde.htm) [When you get your dream job by chance]. *Altandetlige.dk* (in Danish). [University of Copenhagen](/wiki/University_of_Copenhagen "University of Copenhagen"). De økonomiske 'super stars'. Archived from [the original](http://altandetlige.dk/fagligt/ulrikke-ekelund-droemmejobbet-ved-tilfaelde.htm) on 9 January 2015. Retrieved 9 January 2015. "For de kan forklare komplicerede økonomiske emner på en enkel måde, som journalisterne sjældent selv evner. (For they can explain complex economic issues in a simple way that journalists are rarely able to.)"

["Vacancies Offered by Public Service Commission of Kenya"](https://books.google.com/books?id=7Lcb_XlPsUUC&dq=Chief%20economist&pg=PA1095). *[Kenya Gazette](/wiki/Kenya_Gazette "Kenya Gazette")*. **100** (34): 1105. 19 June 1998. ["Office of the Chief Economist"](https://2009-2017.state.gov/e/oce). [United States Department of State](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_State "United States Department of State"). 2 July 2012.

["Leadership"](https://www.usda.gov/oce/about_oce/leadership.htm). *Office of the Chief Economist*. [United States Department of Agriculture](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_Agriculture "United States Department of Agriculture"). Retrieved 4 January 2015.`{{cite web}}`: CS1 maint: deprecated archival service ([link](/wiki/Category:CS1_maint:_deprecated_archival_service "Category:CS1 maint: deprecated archival service"))

["Office of the Chief Economist"](https://2009-2017.state.gov/e/oce). [United States Department of State](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_State "United States Department of State"). Retrieved 4 January 2015.

["Office of the Chief Economist"](https://web.archive.org/web/20190314135926/https://www.state.gov/r/pa/ei/biog/290312.htm). [United States Department of State](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_State "United States Department of State"). Archived from [the original](https://www.state.gov/r/pa/ei/biog/290312.htm) on 14 March 2019. Retrieved 13 May 2019.
A new [Kenyan Constitution](/wiki/Constitution_of_Kenya "Constitution of Kenya") was ratified in 2010, likely significantly revising the roles and organization of the government.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

(Redirected from [Chief Economist](/w/index.php?title=Chief_Economist&redirect=no "Chief Economist"))

A **chief economist** is a single-position job class having primary responsibility for the development, coordination, and production of [economic](/wiki/Economic "Economic") and [financial](/wiki/Financial "Financial") analysis. It is distinguished from the other [economist](/wiki/Economist "Economist") positions by the broader scope of responsibility encompassing the planning, supervision, and coordination of the economic research.

## Synopsis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chief_economist&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Synopsis")]

Chief economists work primarily in [banks](/wiki/Bank "Bank") and [government](/wiki/Government "Government") institutions. By the 2010s, the chief economist position was established at large [internet companies](/wiki/Dot_com_company "Dot com company") such as [Google](/wiki/Google "Google") and [Microsoft](/wiki/Microsoft "Microsoft").[[1]](#cite_note-depillis2014-1) Further, a trend began in data and financial services companies to utilize chief economists in customer- and media-facing roles to help in placing data in a larger economic context.[[1]](#cite_note-depillis2014-1)[[2]](#cite_note-praefke2009-2)

## Government positions

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Chief_economist&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Government positions")]

In the European Union (EU), the post of Chief Economist (*aka* Chief Competition Economist) was created in 2003 as part of the [European Commission](/wiki/European_Commission "European Commission"), reporting to the [Director General for Competition](/w/index.php?title=Director_General_for_Competition&action=edit&redlink=1 "Director General for Competition (page does not exist)").[[3]](#cite_note-gonzdiaz2012-3) This role is primarily focused on advising around the application of EU competition rules and providing input to legal cases before the European Courts.[[3]](#cite_note-gonzdiaz2012-3)

In Kenya, as of 1998[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Chief_economist&action=edit),[[nt 1]](#cite_note-4) the role of chief economist was expressed as "Chief Economist/Statistician" and resided in the [Ministry of Planning and National Development](/w/index.php?title=Ministry_of_Planning_and_National_Development&action=edit&redlink=1 "Ministry of Planning and National Development (page does not exist)"), one responsibility of which was to lead "a planning department or the [Central Bureau of Statistics](/w/index.php?title=Central_Bureau_of_Statistics_(Kenya)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Central Bureau of Statistics (Kenya) (page does not exist)")".[[4]](#cite_note-5)

In the United States government, there are several Offices of the Chief Economist for different departments. For instance, the Office of the Chief Economist within the [United States Department of State](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_State "United States Department of State") was first staffed in March 2012 by [Heidi Crebo-Rediker](/wiki/Heidi_Crebo-Rediker "Heidi Crebo-Rediker"),[[5]](#cite_note-6) and the Office of the Chief Economist within the [United States Department of Agriculture](/wiki/United_States_Department_of_Agriculture "United States Department of Agriculture") is led, as of 2014[[update]](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=Chief_economist&action=edit), by Joseph W. Glauber.[[6]](#cite_note-7) From 2014-2016, the Chief Economist for the U.S. State Department was Rodney D. Ludema.[[7]](#cite_note-8)[[8]](#cite_note-9) The Chief Economist as of January 20, 2019 to present is Dr. [Sharon Brown-Hruska](/wiki/Sharon_Brown-Hruska "Sharon Brown-Hruska").[[9]](#cite_note-10)

In Canada, the Office of the Chief Economist reports and advises on international trade and economic issues, including global, regional and sectoral trends and developments; and undertakes trade and economic research and analysis in support of Foreign Affairs, Trade and Development Canada's various functions: trade and investment policy, including negotiation of international agreements; international business development; and trade litigation. The post-holders are civil servants responsible to the [Minister of International Trade](/wiki/Minister_of_International_Trade "Minister of International Trade").[[10]](#cite_note-11) [Export Development Canada](/wiki/Export_Development_Canada "Export Development Canada") and [Business Development Canada](/w/index.php?title=Business_Development_Canada&action=edit&redlink=1 "Business Development Canada (page does not exist)") both have Chief Economists too.
